name: Phase close + v0.8.0 release preparation (H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE)

design_notes.what_this_feature_is: Phase-006 F-059, the phase's final feature: a PREPARE-ONLY v0.8.0 MINOR release package on the feature-045 pattern (phase 005 / v0.7.0). It rolls CHANGELOG's [Unreleased] into a dated [0.8.0] section, bumps the four version surfaces (NIZAM.json manifest, CONTEXT.md frontmatter, the guide's two anchors, README's release pins), syncs the phase-006 check-count growth 12->15 into the shipped docs, and emits a SHA-pinned release-readiness checklist whose two human items stay PENDING. It does NOT sign off the release and does NOT create the tag -- those are the operator's, per the recorded two-part gate.
design_notes.operator_authorization_verbatim: Rebase line to fit the objective. You are approved to proceed.
design_notes.authorization_interpretation: The operator's message clears the two things that gated F-059: (1) 'Rebase line to fit the objective' authorizes the methodology/00_planning.md Section 9 scope-budget re-baseline (original_estimate_lines 2300 -> 2600; new 130% ceiling 2990 -> 3380), recorded structurally in scope_budget.rebaselines before any implementation; (2) 'You are approved to proceed' authorizes PREPARING this prepare-only package. It is NOT the H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE sign-off: per the 045 two-part gate, checklist item 7 (sign-off) and item 8 (tag) remain at the pinned PENDING literal until the operator executes them after reviewing the PR -- exactly as v0.7.0 was signed off ('I Sign off release-readiness', recorded at 31f3fff before the operator-pushed tag at 4833322).
design_notes.release_tier_classification: MINOR, per 06_release_train.md Section 3.2 -- phase 006 added new OPTIONAL capability (validator checks C13/C14/C15, new verify_lib primitives, new ecosystem_preflight CLI options, an additive `enforcement:` frontmatter field riding additionalProperties:true, and honesty banners) without invalidating consumer content that previously validated. The one schema narrowing -- schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json requiring `blocking_findings` when verdict==FAIL (F-056/NDEBT-024) -- is a TOOL-OUTPUT schema regenerated each run by the same-release ecosystem_preflight.py, not durable consumer-authored content, so no consumer document is invalidated by upgrading; it is disclosed in the [0.8.0] CHANGELOG entry per Section 4 with this rationale rather than rounded up to breaking.
design_notes.the_045_pattern: feature 045 (phase 005) shipped exactly five version surfaces (CHANGELOG.md, NIZAM.json, docs/guide/index.html, CONTEXT.md, README.md) plus .agent/evidence/045-release-readiness-checklist.md, and DEFERRED the ROADMAP/DEBT phase-close narrative to a separate post-release refresh (commit 27f28c3). F-059 mirrors that: it adds tools/README.md + tools/validate.sh to the shipped set ONLY for the honest check-count sync (12->15), and likewise defers the ROADMAP Current-Position / phase-006-COMPLETE narrative and any DEBT phase-close roll to a post-v0.8.0 refresh. The phase-close itself is carried by durable state (run_state phase_complete + feature_list 059 complete), not a doc rewrite.
design_notes.process_honesty: Single-session collapsed-role execution, disclosed-and-permitted by the AGF Orchestrator clause. Per 06_release_train.md Section 6, release mechanics (changelog roll-up, date stamp, tag) do not require a Generator contract and do not pass the Loop 1/Loop 2 harness -- but the phase applies its adversarial-verification discipline anyway, because a botched version bump, a broken CHANGELOG structure, a stale count, or an accidental self-tag are exactly the regressions a release feature must not ship. Anti-vacuity (E01) and confinement (E11) anchor to the phase base 707a3f5 (the F-058 merge, PR #37); the coordination commit recording the operator authorization + the Section-9 re-baseline sits between the base and the deliverable, per the gate-decision-before-acting rule (NDEBT-018.2 / ecosystem/01 4.1).

files_create.purpose: the 045-pattern SHA-pinned release-readiness checklist: items 1-6 cite evidence, items 7 (sign-off) + 8 (tag) held at the pinned PENDING literal.

files_modify.purpose: roll [Unreleased] into a dated [0.8.0] - 2026-07-20 MINOR section (tier + blocking_findings schema-change disclosure); add a fresh empty [Unreleased].
framework.version 0.7.0 -> 0.8.0 (the C10 anchor); validator capability summary C1-C13 -> C1-C15 + C14/C15 descriptions (count sync).
meta framework-version + footer version 0.7.0 -> 0.8.0 (C10 lockstep with NIZAM.json).
frontmatter version 0.7.0 -> 0.8.0 + a matching change_log entry (C8/NDS Sec 4).
full re-pin: every v0.7.0 bootstrap/release reference -> v0.8.0.
count sync: Compliance Coverage C1-C13 -> C1-C15 (thirteen->fifteen, 13 passed -> 15 passed), add C14/C15 table rows; version + change_log bump (C8).
count sync: usage/header comments 'checks C1-C10' and 'all 13 checks (C1-C13)' -> C1-C15 / 'all 15 checks (C1-C15)' (comment/help text only; no logic change).

non_goals: No H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE self-sign-off -- checklist item 7 stays PENDING for the operator (the 'approved to proceed' message authorized PREPARING the package, not signing off the release).
No v0.8.0 tag -- checklist item 8 stays PENDING; the operator cuts the annotated tag at the merge commit (06_release_train.md Sec 2/6; v0.7.0 precedent).

No ROADMAP Current-Position / phase-006-COMPLETE narrative rewrite and no DEBT phase-close roll -- deferred to a post-v0.8.0 refresh, mirroring the 045 -> 27f28c3 precedent; the phase-close is carried by run_state phase_complete + feature_list.
No runtime/behaviour change and no new enforcement -- this is release packaging plus an honest count sync; validate.sh's logic is untouched (only its comment/help text).
No change to any schema, protocol id, or shipped file path -- consistent with the MINOR classification.

verification.acceptance_test: ANTI-VACUITY (base-anchored to 707a3f5 = the F-058 merge): at base NIZAM.json framework.version is 0.7.0 (not 0.8.0); CHANGELOG has no [0.8.0] section; the guide anchors are 0.7.0; tools/README.md coverage reads C1-C13; no 059 release-readiness checklist exists.
verification.command:
set -e
base=707a3f5
v=$(git show $base:NIZAM.json | python3 -c 'import json,sys;print(json.load(sys.stdin)["framework"]["version"])')
test "$v" = "0.7.0" || { echo "VACUITY: base version not 0.7.0 ($v)"; exit 1; }
if git show $base:CHANGELOG.md | grep -Fq '## [0.8.0]'; then echo "VACUITY: base already has [0.8.0]"; exit 1; fi
if git show $base:docs/guide/index.html | grep -Fq 'content="0.8.0"'; then echo "VACUITY: guide already 0.8.0 at base"; exit 1; fi
git show $base:tools/README.md | grep -Fq 'C1-C13' || git show $base:tools/README.md | grep -Fq $'C1–C13' || { echo "VACUITY: base tools/README already not C1-C13"; exit 1; }
if git cat-file -e $base:.agent/evidence/059-release-readiness-checklist.md 2>/dev/null; then echo "VACUITY: 059 checklist exists at base"; exit 1; fi
echo "E01 PASS: at base 707a3f5 version=0.7.0, no [0.8.0] CHANGELOG, guide not 0.8.0, README C1-C13, no 059 checklist (anti-vacuity)"

verification.acceptance_test: CHANGELOG STRUCTURE (roll-up): the first three '## [' headings are [Unreleased] (empty), then [0.8.0] - 2026-07-20, then [0.7.0]; the [Unreleased] section carries no ### subsection (the entries rolled into [0.8.0]).

CHANGELOG CONTENT + TIER: the [0.8.0] section names the MINOR tier, preserves the rolled-up phase-006 constitutional entry, and carries the schema-narrowing (blocking_findings) minor-not-breaking rationale required by 06_release_train.md Section 4.
NIZAM.json VERSION -- STRICT SEMVER INCREASE (JSON-parsed): framework.version is 0.8.0 and a strict tuple increase over the base 0.7.0.
verification.command:
set -e
head=$(git show 707a3f5:NIZAM.json | python3 -c 'import json,sys;print(json.load(sys.stdin)["framework"]["version"])')
now=$(python3 -c 'import json;print(json.load(open("NIZAM.json"))["framework"]["version"])')
test "$now" = "0.8.0" || { echo "NIZAM.json version not 0.8.0 ($now)"; exit 1; }
python3 -c "
def t(v): return tuple(int(x) for x in v.split('.'))
assert t('$now') > t('$head'), 'not a strict increase: $head -> $now'
print('$head -> $now strict semver increase')"
echo "E04 PASS: NIZAM.json 0.7.0 -> 0.8.0 (strict semver increase)"

verification.acceptance_test: GUIDE VERSION ANCHORS (C10 External Anchor Rule): the guide's meta framework-version AND footer version both read 0.8.0 and equal NIZAM.json framework.version.
CONTEXT.md VERSION + CHANGE_LOG (C8/NDS Sec 4): the frontmatter version is 0.8.0, a strict increase over base, and the frontmatter change_log carries a matching 0.8.0 entry.
README.md -- FULL RE-PIN: zero v0.7.0 occurrences remain and the bootstrap/release references now pin v0.8.0.

RELEASE-READINESS CHECKLIST (045 pattern): the checklist has 8 numbered items; items 1-6 each cite an evidence path/artifact; items 7 (sign-off) and 8 (tag) are held at the pinned literal 'PENDING -- human-gated (H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE)'.
NO-SELF-TAG INVARIANT: no v0.8.0 git tag has been created by this feature (the pipeline never self-tags; the operator cuts the tag at merge per 06_release_train.md Sec 2/6).
verification.command:
set -e
if git tag | grep -qE '^v0\.8\.0$'; then echo "VIOLATION: a v0.8.0 tag exists"; exit 1; fi
echo "E09 PASS: no v0.8.0 tag exists at close (the pipeline never self-tags)"

verification.acceptance_test: REGRESSION (rc-captured): default sweep 15/15, payload 11/11, fixtures self-test OK, e2e bootstrap green -- each rc asserted.
verification.command:
set -e
out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0; printf '%s\n' "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Fxq 'SUMMARY: 15 passed, 0 failed'
out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --payload 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0; printf '%s\n' "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Fxq 'SUMMARY (payload mode): 11 passed, 0 failed'
out=$(bash tools/fixtures_self_test.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0; printf '%s\n' "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Fq 'SELF-TEST OK'
bash tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "e2e failed"; exit 1; }
echo "E10 PASS: sweep 15/15, payload 11/11, self-test OK, e2e green (rc=0 each)"

verification.acceptance_test: DELIVERABLE CONFINEMENT: the non-.agent changes across 707a3f5..HEAD are exactly the 7 contracted files.
verification.command:
set -e
changed=$(git diff --name-only 707a3f5..HEAD | grep -v '^\.agent/' | LC_ALL=C sort | tr '\n' ' ')
expected='CHANGELOG.md CONTEXT.md NIZAM.json README.md docs/guide/index.html tools/README.md tools/validate.sh '
test "$changed" = "$expected" || { echo "confinement mismatch:"; echo " got:      [$changed]"; echo " expected: [$expected]"; exit 1; }
echo "E11 PASS: non-.agent changes are exactly the 7 contracted files"

verification.acceptance_test: DOCS/COUNT SYNC: the phase-006 sweep growth 12->15 is reflected -- NIZAM.json validator summary reads C1-C15, tools/README.md coverage reads fifteen/15 passed/C1-C15 with C14 and C15 table rows, and validate.sh usage no longer states the stale 'all 13 checks (C1-C13)' or 'checks C1-C10'.

OPERATOR AUTHORIZATION + SECTION-9 RE-BASELINE RECORDED: run_state carries the verbatim operator authorization for F-059 (H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE prep) and a structural scope_budget rebaseline {from:2300,to:2600,authorized_by:operator} with original_estimate_lines raised to 2600.
verification.command:
set -e
python3 - <<'PY'
import json
rs=json.load(open('.agent/run_state.json'))
sb=rs['scope_budget']
assert sb['original_estimate_lines']==2600, sb['original_estimate_lines']
rb=sb['rebaselines'][-1]
assert rb['from']==2300 and rb['to']==2600, rb
assert rb['authorized_by']=='operator' and 'reason' in rb and 'at' in rb, rb
ev=[h for h in rs['history'] if h.get('event')=='operator_gate_decision' and 'H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE' in h.get('detail','') and '059' in h.get('detail','')]
assert ev, 'no F-059 H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE authorization event'
d=ev[-1]['detail']
assert 'Rebase line to fit the objective' in d and 'approved to proceed' in d, 'verbatim authorization not recorded'
print('run_state: rebaseline 2300->2600 (operator) + verbatim F-059 authorization recorded')
PY
echo "E13 PASS: Section-9 re-baseline (2300->2600) + verbatim operator authorization recorded in run_state"

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured output is in .agent/evidence/059-verify-NN.txt, plus the consolidated .agent/evidence/059/verification.txt and 059/diff.patch (git diff 707a3f5..HEAD). Commands run from the repo root; anti-vacuity (E01) and confinement (E11) anchor to the phase base 707a3f5 (the F-058 merge, PR #37), since the coordination commit recording the operator authorization + the Section-9 re-baseline sits between the base and this deliverable.

approvals.process_note: Operator-gated feature (H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE, two-part). The operator verbatim authorization (design_notes.operator_authorization_verbatim) is recorded in the run_state coordination commit before implementation and re-verified by E13. Per 06_release_train.md Sec 6 the release mechanics need no Generator contract; the phase applies its verification discipline regardless. Items 7-8 remain PENDING by design; the operator executes them after PR review, and QA record .agent/qa/059.json documents the independent re-run.
